description: Meals and work can gather around 'wonepa', a supported, mostly flat surface arranged for use or for placing objects. Tables may be high or low and made from any suitable material. 'meipa' (seat) supports a sitting body; a table supports the activity or objects before that body.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips for voiced 'w' and move into mid-back 'o'. Place the tongue tip at the upper teeth for voiced dental nasal 'n', release into stressed mid-front 'e', then close both lips for unvoiced 'p' and finish on open 'a'.
usage_notes: The noun refers to furniture or a comparable physical work surface. A tabular 'sirami' (record) retains its source layout when that arrangement carries data.

semantic_domains.activity: a shared or individual surface arranged for meals, work, or meeting
semantic_domains.physical: a supported flat surface that holds objects during use
